purpose: Per-parser-family AST-shape contract for the SystemVerilog grammar. The generated SV parser is NOT in the default `cargo test --features generated_parsers` build; it is produced on-demand by `sv_stimuli_quality_gate` (and similar) into `rust/target/<gate>/work/systemverilog_parser.rs` and discarded. The cfg `has_generated_systemverilog_parser` therefore stays off in default builds and the matching unit test compiles out. When the parser is present (gate run or `PGEN_SYSTEMVERILOG_PARSER_PATH` override), the test activates and the contract is enforced.
doctrine: Calibrated 2026-05-04 against a freshly-generated parser (parser release 1.0.1, contract 1.0.1). On first run with the parser available, this manifest is expected to match — `current_content_kind: "json_object"` was confirmed by running `parseability_probe --parse-dump-ast-pretty systemverilog /tmp/sv_calibration/minimal_module.sv` after the SV-Slice-1 grammar fix that moved the dangling `systemverilog_file` annotation onto the rule it was supposed to attach to. Future drift fails the regression-lock and surfaces the change to the slice that introduced it.
calibration_history: 2026-05-06 SV-Slice-36 (batch): assignments + procedural assertions + randcase typed (8 rules / 16 annotations) — closes 4 more statement_item kinds (nonblocking_assignment / procedural_continuous_assignment / clocking_drive / randcase / procedural_assertion). (a) `nonblocking_assignment` typed `{lvalue, control, value}` (drops less_equal). (b) `procedural_continuous_assignment` 6 kinds: `"assign"` `{body}` (kw_assign + variable_assignment) / `"deassign"` `{target}` / `"force_variable"` `{body}` / `"force_net"` `{body}` / `"release_variable"` `{target}` / `"release_net"` `{target}`. (c) `clocking_drive` typed `{lvalue, cycle_delay, value}` (drops less_equal). (d) `randcase_statement` typed `{items: {first, rest}}` (drops kw_randcase / kw_endcase; mini-mixed-array). (e) `randcase_item` typed `{weight, body}` (drops colon). (f) `procedural_assertion_statement` 3 kinds (concurrent / immediate / checker_instantiation). (g) `immediate_assertion_statement` 2 kinds (simple / deferred — bridges to deferred_immediate_assertion_statement typed in SV-Slice-30). (h) `variable_assignment` typed `{lvalue, value}` (drops assign). DEFERRED: `blocking_assignment_sv_2017/2023` typing — branch 2 has parens-Or `( implicit_class_handle dot | class_scope | package_scope )?` (3-way Or with mixed sequence/atom bodies); needs helper-rule extraction. Annotation count: 473 (was 457, +16). Same accept set.

2026-05-06 SV-Slice-35 (batch): conditional_statement typed via helper-rule extraction (1 rule / 1 annotation + 1 new helper rule with 2 annotations) — closes the if-else dispatch path. Unblocks the SV-Slice-34 DEFERRED `conditional_statement` typing using the established helper-rule pattern (third use after if_generate_else_clause from SV-Slice-23 and net_strength/net_vector_scalar from SV-Slice-26). (a) `conditional_statement` typed `{unique_priority, condition, then_body, else_body}` (drops kw_if / lparen / rparen / kw_else; the original parens-Or `( conditional_statement | statement_or_null )` extracted to helper rule). (b) `conditional_else_branch` (NEW helper rule) 2 kinds: `"elseif"` (body is recursive conditional_statement; supports `else if (...)` chains) / `"else"` (body is statement_or_null; terminal else). Cascading walk impact: every reachable `statement_item.kind=='conditional'` (typed in SV-Slice-32) now exposes typed dispatch into condition / then-body / else-body. NOTE: the rule preserves a `&kw_else` positive lookahead before the consumed `kw_else` — this is a PEG idiom from the source grammar that asserts the else-branch is required; it appears unchanged in the helper-rule refactor. Annotation count: 457 (was 454, +3). Same accept set.

2026-05-06 SV-Slice-34 (batch): case + loop families typed (7 rules / 18 annotations) — closes case-statement and loop-statement walks (statement_item kinds 'case' and 'loop'). (a) `case_statement` typed `{unique_priority, keyword, expr, items: {first, rest}}` (drops kw_endcase, parens; mini-mixed-array on items). (b) `case_keyword` 3 kinds bare (case / casez / casex). (c) `case_item` 2 kinds (expr_list `{exprs: {first, rest}, body}` / default `{body}`). (d) `case_pattern_item` 2 kinds (pattern `{pattern, condition, body}` / default `{body}` — condition is the optional `&&& expression` guard). (e) `case_inside_item_sv_2017` 2 kinds (range_list `{ranges, body}` using open_range_list / default `{body}`). (f) `case_inside_item_sv_2023` 2 kinds (range_list using LRM 2023 `range_list` instead of `open_range_list` / default). (g) `loop_statement` 6 kinds: `"forever"` `{body}` / `"repeat"` `{count, body}` / `"while"` `{condition, body}` / `"for"` `{init, condition, step, body}` / `"do_while"` `{body, condition}` / `"foreach"` `{array, loop_vars, body}`. DEFERRED: `unique_priority` typing — has duplicate `kw_unique` branches (probable grammar bug, branches 0 and 1 identical), needs grammar fix before clean annotation. DEFERRED: `conditional_statement` typing — has parens-Or `( conditional_statement | statement_or_null )` and `&kw_else` lookahead pattern requiring careful helper-rule extraction; tracked for future slice. Annotation count: 454 (was 436, +18). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-33 (batch): procedural-statement forms typed (11 rules / 26 annotations) — closes 7 statement_item kinds. (a) `disable_statement` 3 kinds (task / block / fork). (b) `jump_statement` 3 kinds (return `{value}` / break / continue). (c) `wait_statement` 3 kinds (wait `{condition, body}` / wait_fork / wait_order `{events: {first, rest}, action}`). (d) `event_trigger_sv_2017` 2 kinds (non_blocking `{name}` / blocking `{control, name}`). (e) `event_trigger_sv_2023` 2 kinds (parallel; both add `select` field per LRM 2023 nonrange_select). (f) `procedural_timing_control_statement` typed `{control, body}`. (g) `procedural_timing_control` 3 kinds (delay / event / cycle). (h) `subroutine_call` 5 kinds (class_scoped_tf / tf / system_tf / method / randomize with optional std-scope prefix). (i) `subroutine_call_statement` 2 kinds (call / void_cast for void function calls). (j) `seq_block` typed `{label, declarations, statements, end_label}`. (k) `par_block` typed `{label, declarations, statements, join, end_label}` — `join` carries the typed `join_keyword`. Cascading walk impact: 7 of statement_item's 19/20 kinds (disable/jump/wait/event_trigger/procedural_timing_control/subroutine_call/par_block/seq_block) now expose typed dispatch into actual content. Annotation count: 436 (was 410, +26). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-32 (batch): statement_item dispatch typed (3 rules / 43 annotations — crosses 400-annotation milestone). Closes the statement.body field, exposing typed dispatch into all 20 procedural-statement forms (or 19 in sv_2023). (a) `statement_item_sv_2017` 20 kinds: blocking_assignment / nonblocking_assignment / procedural_continuous_assignment (each drops trailing semi) / case / conditional / inc_or_dec_expression (drops semi) / subroutine_call / disable / event_trigger / loop / jump / par_block / procedural_timing_control / seq_block / wait / procedural_assertion / clocking_drive (drops semi) / randsequence / randcase / expect_property. (b) `statement_item_sv_2023` 19 kinds (same as sv_2017 except `inc_or_dec_expression semi` is removed — LRM 2023 disallows bare-statement increment/decrement at procedural level since the same semantics are subsumed by blocking_assignment with `++`/`--`). (c) `block_item_declaration` 4 kinds (block_data / local_parameter / parameter / let) — each preserves attribute_instance* prefix; reached from tf_item_declaration.kind=='block_item' (typed in SV-Slice-31). After this slice, every framed statement in module/program/function/task bodies exposes typed dispatch into actual procedural-statement form. Crosses 400-annotation threshold. Annotation count: 410 (was 367, +43). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-31 (batch): action_block + statement framing typed (5 rules / 9 annotations) — closes the action_block walk path used pervasively by assertions and conditional statements. (a) `action_block` 2 kinds: `"always"` (`{body}` — single statement_or_null, runs unconditionally) / `"with_else"` (`{pass, fail}` — pass is optional pre-else statement, fail is the else statement_or_null; LRM A.6.3 form `[statement] else statement_or_null`). (b) `statement` typed `{label, attributes, body}` (label is optional `( block_identifier colon !colon )?` — the `!colon` is a negative lookahead distinguishing block-label from `::` scope-resolution; attributes is the standard attribute_instance* prefix). (c) `statement_or_null` 2 kinds: `"statement"` (`{body}` — wraps a non-null statement) / `"null"` (`{attributes}` — bare `;` with optional preceding attribute_instance*). (d) `function_statement_or_null` parallel 2 kinds — same shape as statement_or_null but for function bodies. (e) `tf_item_declaration` 2 kinds (block_item / tf_port) — referenced from function_body_declaration.items / task_body_declaration.items. After this slice, every assertion's `action`/`statement` field, every function/task body item, and every framed statement now exposes typed dispatch into actual content. Annotation count: 367 (was 358, +9). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-30 (batch): deferred immediate assertions typed (5 rules / 10 annotations) — closes assertion_item.kind=='deferred_immediate' walk path. (a) `deferred_immediate_assertion_item` typed `{label, body}` — label is the optional `( block_identifier colon )?` per LRM A.6.10 (parallel to concurrent_assertion_item.kind='statement' label slot). (b) `deferred_immediate_assertion_statement` 3 kinds (assert / assume / cover) — uniform `{kind, body}` dispatch. (c) `deferred_immediate_assert_statement` 2 kinds: `"zero_delay"` (`{expression, action}` — `assert #0 (expr) action_block`) / `"final"` (`{expression, action}` — `assert final (expr) action_block`). (d) `deferred_immediate_assume_statement` parallel 2 kinds (zero_delay / final) — same shape as assert. (e) `deferred_immediate_cover_statement` parallel 2 kinds (zero_delay / final) — uses `statement` field (statement_or_null) instead of `action` since cover has no pass/fail branching. After this slice, both `assertion_item.kind=='concurrent'` (typed in SV-Slice-29) and `assertion_item.kind=='deferred_immediate'` (typed THIS slice) walk paths are fully closed. Annotation count: 358 (was 348, +10). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-29 (batch): concurrent assertion + constraint family typed (16 rules / 28 annotations). Closes assertion_item.kind=='concurrent' and class_constraint walk paths. Concurrent assertions: (a) `concurrent_assertion_statement` 5 kinds (assert_property / assume_property / cover_property / cover_sequence / restrict_property). (b) `assert_property_statement` typed `{spec, action}` (drops kw_assert / kw_property / parens). (c) `assume_property_statement` parallel `{spec, action}`. (d) `cover_property_statement` `{spec, statement}` (cover uses statement_or_null instead of action_block). (e) `cover_sequence_statement` `{clocking, disable_iff, sequence, statement}` (drops kw_cover/kw_sequence/parens). (f) `restrict_property_statement` `{spec}` only (no action — restrict is just a property assertion that prunes formal traces). (g) `expect_property_statement` `{spec, action}`. Constraints: (h) `constraint_declaration_sv_2017` `{static_keyword, name, block}`. (i) `constraint_declaration_sv_2023` `{static_keyword, dynamic_override, name, block}` (LRM 2023 dynamic-override addition, parallel to function/task). (j) `constraint_block` `{items}` (drops braces). (k) `constraint_block_item` 2 kinds (`"solve_before"` `{before, after}` / `"expression"` `{body}`). (l) `constraint_expression` 6 kinds (expression with optional soft / uniqueness / implies / if / foreach / disable_soft) — each with structured fields. (m) `constraint_prototype_sv_2017` `{qualifier, static_keyword, name}`. (n) `constraint_prototype_sv_2023` `{qualifier, static_keyword, dynamic_override, name}`. (o) `constraint_prototype_qualifier` 2 kinds bare {kind} (extern / pure). (p) `constraint_set` 2 kinds (single / block). Cascading walk impact: assertion_item.body now exposes typed dispatch into all 5 concurrent assertion forms; class_constraint.body exposes typed declaration / prototype dispatch with full constraint expression type discrimination. Annotation count: 348 (was 320, +28). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-28 (batch): class qualifiers typed (3 rules / 6 annotations) — completes SV-Slice-27's class body picture. (a) `method_qualifier` 2 kinds: `"virtual"` (`{pure}` — preserves the optional `( kw_pure )?` slot; `[]` when bare virtual, `[<kw_pure>]` when pure virtual) / `"class_item_qualifier"` (`{body}` — wraps the typed class_item_qualifier shape). (b) `property_qualifier` 2 kinds: `"random"` (`{body}` — wraps random_qualifier) / `"class_item_qualifier"` (`{body}` — same as method_qualifier). (c) `random_qualifier` 2 kinds (bare `{kind}`): `"rand"` / `"randc"`. Cascading walk impact: every reachable `class_method.qualifiers[]` and `class_property.qualifiers[]` (typed in SV-Slice-27 with `qualifiers: $1` field) now exposes typed dispatch. Annotation count: 320 (was 314, +6). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-27 (batch): class body sub-tree typed (6 rules / 30 annotations). Closes the class body walk path from class_declaration.items. (a) `class_item_sv_2017` 8 kinds (property / method / constraint / class / covergroup — first 5 with attribute_instance* prefix; local_parameter / parameter / semi). (b) `class_item_sv_2023` 9 kinds (same plus interface_class branch — LRM 2023 nested interface-class declarations inside classes). (c) `class_item_qualifier` 3 kinds (bare {kind}: static / protected / local). (d) `class_constraint` 2 kinds (prototype / declaration). (e) `class_property` 2 kinds: `"decl"` ({qualifiers, body} — wraps data_declaration with property_qualifier* prefix) / `"const"` ({qualifiers, data_type, name, init} — kw_const-prefixed constant property declaration). (f) `class_method` 6 kinds: task / function / pure_virtual / extern / constructor / extern_constructor — each preserves method_qualifier* or class_item_qualifier* prefix appropriately. Cascading walk impact: every reachable `class_declaration_sv_2017/2023.items[]` now exposes typed dispatch into class members; method qualifiers (static/protected/local), property kind (decl vs const), and method kind (task vs function vs pure_virtual etc.) all directly accessible. Annotation count: 314 (was 284, +30). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-26 (batch): net_declaration typed via helper-rule extraction (4 rules / 10 annotations + 2 new helper rules). Closes the net_declaration walk path from data_declaration (sv_2017) and module_common_item.kind='net_alias' contexts. (a) `net_declaration_sv_2017` 3 kinds: `"wire"` (`{net_type, strength, vector_scalar, data_type, delay, assignments}`) / `"alias"` (`{net_type_id, delay_control, assignments}`) / `"interconnect"` (`{data_type, delay, name, dims, second}`). (b) `net_declaration_sv_2023` 3 kinds (same structure; alias branch uses `nettype_id` instead of `net_type_id` per LRM 2023). (c) `net_strength` (NEW helper rule) 2 kinds: `"drive"` (`{body: drive_strength shape}`) / `"charge"` (`{body: charge_strength shape}`) — extracted from inline `( drive_strength | charge_strength )?` to dodge task #38. (d) `net_vector_scalar` (NEW helper rule) 2 kinds (bare `{kind}`): `"vectored"` / `"scalared"` — extracted from inline `( kw_vectored | kw_scalared )?`. The wire branch's `strength` and `vector_scalar` fields use the typed helper rules so consumers can dispatch by kind without descending into raw token envelopes. Annotation count: 284 (was 274, +10). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-25 (batch): data/function/task declarations + bodies typed (8 rules / 14 annotations) — closes data/function/task walk paths from package_or_generate_item_declaration. (a) `data_declaration_sv_2017` 4 kinds (variable_decl with `{const_keyword, var_keyword, lifetime, data_type, assignments}` field set / type / package_import / net_type). (b) `data_declaration_sv_2023` 4 kinds (same 3 as sv_2017 plus `nettype` instead of `net_type` — LRM 2023 nettype-vs-net_type naming). (c) `function_declaration_sv_2017` typed `{lifetime, body}` (drops kw_function). (d) `function_declaration_sv_2023` typed `{dynamic_override, lifetime, body}` (LRM 2023 adds `( dynamic_override_specifiers )?` slot before lifetime). (e) `task_declaration_sv_2017` typed `{lifetime, body}` (drops kw_task). (f) `task_declaration_sv_2023` typed `{dynamic_override, lifetime, body}` (same LRM 2023 dynamic-override addition). (g) `function_body_declaration` typed `{return_type, name, items, statements, end_label}` (drops semi, kw_endfunction). (h) `task_body_declaration` typed `{name, items, statements, end_label}` (no return_type — task is void). DEFERRED: `net_declaration_sv_2017/2023` typing — has parens-Or `( drive_strength | charge_strength )?` and `( kw_vectored | kw_scalared )?` that hit task #38; would need helper-rule extraction following the SV-Slice-23 pattern (planned for next slice). Cascading walk impact: every reachable `package_or_generate_item_declaration.kind=='data_declaration'`/`'function_declaration'`/`'task_declaration'` now exposes typed dispatch all the way to the body. Annotation count: 274 (was 260, +14). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-24 (batch): assertion + genvar dispatch typed (7 rules / 26 annotations). (a) `assertion_item` 2 kinds (concurrent / deferred_immediate) — uniform `{kind, body}`. (b) `assertion_item_declaration` 3 kinds (property / sequence / let). (c) `concurrent_assertion_item` 2 kinds: `"statement"` (`{label, body}` — label is the optional `( block_identifier colon )?` prefix) / `"checker_instantiation"` (`{body}`). (d) `genvar_initialization` typed `{genvar_keyword, name, value}` — drops `assign`; `genvar_keyword` is `[]` when absent or `[kw_genvar token]` when present. (e) `genvar_iteration` 3 kinds: `"assign"` (`{name, op, value}` — op is the typed assignment_operator), `"prefix_inc_dec"` (`{op, name}`), `"postfix_inc_dec"` (`{name, op}`). (f) `assignment_operator` 13 kinds (assign / plus_assign / minus_assign / star_assign / slash_assign / percent_assign / and_assign / or_assign / xor_assign / shift_left_assign / shift_right_assign / arithmetic_shift_left_assign / arithmetic_shift_right_assign) — uniform `{kind}` shape, no body since each branch is a single keyword token. (g) `inc_or_dec_operator` 2 kinds (plus_plus / minus_minus) — same `{kind}` pattern. Cascading walk impact: every reachable `module_common_item.kind=='assertion_item'` and the loop_generate_construct.init/step fields (typed in SV-Slice-23) now expose typed dispatch into actual content. Annotation count: 260 (was 234, +26). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-23 (batch): generate-construct internals typed (6 rules / 9 annotations + 1 new helper rule). (a) `loop_generate_construct` typed `{init, condition, step, block}` (drops kw_for, lparen, rparen, semis). (b) `conditional_generate_construct` 2 kinds (if / case) — uniform `{kind, body}` dispatch. (c) `if_generate_construct` typed `{condition, then_block, else_clause}` — uses NEW helper rule `if_generate_else_clause` to dodge task #38 (parens-grouped-Or trailing-annotation attribution bug). The original inline `( kw_else if_generate_construct | kw_else generate_block )?` was extracted to a named rule. (d) `if_generate_else_clause` (NEW rule) 2 kinds: `"elseif"` (recursive — body is itself an if_generate_construct, supports `else if` chains) / `"else_block"` (body is a generate_block, terminal). (e) `case_generate_construct` typed `{expr, items: {first, rest}}` (drops kw_case, parens, kw_endcase; mini-mixed-array on items). (f) `case_generate_item` 2 kinds: `"expr_list"` (`{exprs: {first, rest}, block}` — N-way label list with mini-mixed-array) / `"default"` (`{block}` — drops kw_default and optional colon). The new helper rule pattern is RECOMMENDED for any future similar `( a | b )?` / `( a | b )*` parens-Or patterns until task #38 is fixed. Cascading walk impact: every `module_common_item.kind=='loop_generate_construct'` and `'conditional_generate_construct'` now exposes typed dispatch all the way to the generate body. Annotation count: 234 (was 225, +9). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-22 (batch): generate sub-tree typed (3 rules / 7 annotations) — closes the generate-construct walk path. (a) `generate_region` typed `{items: $2}` (drops kw_generate / kw_endgenerate; surfaces clean items array). (b) `generate_item` 3 kinds (module_or_generate_item / interface_or_generate_item / checker_or_generate_item) — uniform `{kind, body}` dispatch on which generate-item form was matched. (c) `generate_block` 3 kinds: `"anonymous"` (`{label, items, end_label}` for `begin ... end` form), `"labeled"` (`{name, label, items, end_label}` for `name : begin ... end` form), `"generate_item"` (`{body}` for the bare-generate_item passthrough — no begin/end wrapping). The optional inner `( colon generate_block_identifier )?` after kw_begin captures the block-internal label slot (or `[]` when absent); the trailing optional captures the end-label slot. Cascading walk impact: every `non_port_module_item.kind=='generate_region'` and every `module_common_item.kind=='loop_generate_construct'`/`'conditional_generate_construct'` (when those typed rules eventually resolve through generate_region or generate_block) now exposes typed dispatch into the generate body. Annotation count: 225 (was 218, +7). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-21 (batch): module_common_item + package_or_generate_item_declaration typed (4 rules / 55 annotations — biggest batch yet). (a) `module_common_item_sv_2017` 13 kinds (module_or_generate_item_declaration / interface_instantiation / program_instantiation / assertion_item / bind_directive / continuous_assign / net_alias / initial_construct / final_construct / always_construct / loop_generate_construct / conditional_generate_construct / elaboration_system_task) — uniform `{kind, body}` shape. (b) `module_common_item_sv_2023` 13 kinds (same as sv_2017 except `elaboration_severity_system_task` instead of `elaboration_system_task` — LRM 2023 system-task expansion). (c) `package_or_generate_item_declaration_sv_2017` 14 kinds (local_parameter_declaration / parameter_declaration / net_declaration / dpi_import_export / data_declaration / task_declaration / function_declaration / checker_declaration / extern_constraint_declaration / class_declaration / class_constructor_declaration / covergroup_declaration / assertion_item_declaration / semi) — `local_parameter_declaration` and `parameter_declaration` drop trailing semi via `body: $1`; `semi` is bare with no body. (d) `package_or_generate_item_declaration_sv_2023` 15 kinds (same plus `interface_class_declaration` between class_declaration and class_constructor_declaration — LRM 2023 interface-class addition). The `module_common_item` and `package_or_generate_item_declaration` profile-tag wrapper rules stay un-annotated (transparent profile-routers, same pattern as `module_declaration`/`interface_declaration`). Cascading walk impact: every `module_or_generate_item.kind=='module_common_item'` and every `non_port_program_item.kind=='module_or_generate_item_declaration'` now exposes typed dispatch through 6+ levels. Annotation count: 218 (was 163, +55). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-20 (batch): interface + program items dispatch tree typed (5 rules / 19 annotations) — interface/program counterparts of SV-Slice-19's module-items batch. (a) `interface_item` 2 kinds (port_declaration / non_port_item). (b) `interface_or_generate_item` 2 kinds (module_common_item / extern_tf_declaration) — each preserves attribute_instance* prefix. (c) `non_port_interface_item` 6 kinds (generate_region / interface_or_generate / program_declaration / modport_declaration / interface_declaration / timeunits_declaration). (d) `program_item` 2 kinds (port_declaration / non_port_item). (e) `non_port_program_item` 7 kinds (continuous_assign / module_or_generate_item_declaration / initial_construct / final_construct / concurrent_assertion_item / timeunits_declaration / program_generate_item) — first 5 preserve attribute_instance* prefix. The interface and program declaration sub-trees now match the module sub-tree's level of typed dispatch — every `header.items` / `body.items` field on every typed interface/program declaration surfaces kind-discriminated dispatch into actual content. Annotation count: 163 (was 144, +19). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-19 (batch): module-items dispatch tree typed (5 rules / 22 annotations). (a) `module_item` 2 kinds (port_declaration / non_port_item). (b) `module_or_generate_item` 5 kinds (parameter_override / gate_instantiation / udp_instantiation / module_instantiation / module_common_item) — each preserves attribute_instance* prefix. (c) `module_or_generate_item_declaration` 5 kinds (package_or_generate / genvar / clocking / default_clocking / default_disable_iff). (d) `non_port_module_item` 8 kinds (generate_region / module_or_generate / specify_block / specparam_declaration / program_declaration / module_declaration / interface_declaration / timeunits_declaration). (e) `continuous_assign` 2 kinds (net / variable). All `header.items` and `body.items` fields on every typed module/interface/program declaration now surface kind-discriminated dispatch into the actual content. Annotation count: 144 (was 122, +22). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-18 (batch): UDP truth-table entry rules typed (3 rules / 3 annotations). (a) `combinational_entry` typed `{inputs, output}` (drops colon/semi). (b) `sequential_entry` typed `{inputs, current_state, next_state}` (drops colons/semi). (c) `udp_initial_statement` typed `{name, init_val}` (drops kw_initial/assign/semi). All UDP truth-table rows now have clean typed shapes; consumers walk inputs/output/state-transitions directly without descending the raw envelope. Annotation count: 122 (was 119, +3). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-17 (batch): UDP body sub-tree typed (6 rules / 8 annotations). (a) `udp_body` 2 kinds (combinational/sequential). (b) `udp_input_declaration` typed `{attributes, identifiers}` (drops kw_input). (c) `udp_output_declaration` 2 kinds (wire / reg with optional default). (d) `combinational_body` typed `{entries: {first, rest}}` (drops kw_table/kw_endtable). (e) `sequential_body` typed `{initial, entries: {first, rest}}` (preserves optional initial statement). (f) `list_of_udp_port_identifiers` typed `{first, rest}` (mini-mixed-array). All UDP declaration internals now typed end-to-end. Annotation count: 119 (was 111, +8). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-16 (batch): port + port_direction + package_import family typed (9 annotations across 4 rules). (a) `port` 2 kinds (expression / named) — discriminates positional vs named-dot port forms. (b) `port_direction` 4 kinds (input/output/inout/ref). (c) `package_import_declaration` `{items: {first, rest}}` — drops kw_import/semi. (d) `package_import_item` 2 kinds (explicit with `{package, name}` / wildcard with `{package}` only). DEFERRED: `ansi_port_declaration` 3-branch typing — task #38 (parens-grouped-Or trailing-annotation attribution bug) blocks it; branch 0 starts with `( net_port_header | interface_port_header )?` which causes branch-index renumbering and lost annotation registration. Would need either task #38 fix or grammar refactor (extract parens-Or into a named helper rule). Annotation count: 111 (was 102, +9). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-15 (batch): port-list family + small structural rules typed (6 rules, 7 annotations). (a) `list_of_ports` typed `{first, rest}` (mini-mixed-array; drops parens). (b) `list_of_port_declarations` transparent passthrough `-> $2` (drops parens, surfaces optional inner content). (c) `udp_port_list` typed `{output, inputs: {first, rest}}` (output_port_identifier + input list with mini-mixed-array). (d) `udp_declaration_port_list` parallel shape `{output, inputs: {first, rest}}` for ANSI form. (e) `anonymous_program` typed `{items}` (drops kw_program/semi/kw_endprogram). (f) `package_export_declaration` 2 kinds (wildcard / explicit with items: {first, rest}). All `header.ports` fields on module/interface/program/UDP declarations now surface typed shapes. Annotation count: 102 (was 95, +7). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-14 (batch): 5 rules typed completing bind sub-tree + interface_class_declaration + config_declaration. (a) `bind_target_scope` 2 kinds (module/interface) — uniform `{kind, name}` shape. (b) `bind_target_instance` typed `{name, bit_select}`. (c) `bind_target_instance_list` typed via {first, rest} mini-mixed-array workaround for `bind_target_instance ( comma bind_target_instance )*` repetition. (d) `interface_class_declaration` typed `{name, parameters, extends, items, end_label}` (single sequence). (e) `config_declaration` typed `{name, local_params, design, rules, end_label}` (single sequence). Annotation count: 95 (was 89, +6). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-13 (batch): bind_directive + bind_instantiation + package_item per-branch typed. (a) `bind_directive` 2 kinds (scoped/single) — scoped form preserves target_scope, optional instances, instantiation; single form preserves target_instance, instantiation. (b) `bind_instantiation` 4 kinds (program/module/interface/checker) — uniform `{kind, body}` shape over the 4 instantiation forms. (c) `package_item` 4 kinds (declaration/anonymous_program/export/timeunits) — discriminates the 4 forms reachable inside `description.body` when description.kind=='package_item'. Annotation count: 89 (was 79, +10). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-12 (batch): UDP declaration family typed (4 rules; mirrors module/interface/program structural pattern with one wrinkle). (a) `udp_ansi_declaration` typed `{attributes, name, ports}` (drops kw_primitive, parens, semi). (b) `udp_nonansi_declaration` same field names (only ports source rule differs). (c) `udp_declaration_sv_2017` 5 per-branch kinds (nonansi/ansi/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi/wildcard). (d) `udp_declaration_sv_2023` mirror with positional shift in wildcard. **Special: nonansi branch has `udp_port_declaration udp_port_declaration*` mini-mixed-array** (a required first + repeat) — used `port_decls: {first: $2, rest: $3}` workaround (same idiom as attribute_instance from slice 6). Annotation count: 79 (was 67, +12). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-11 (batch): program-header sub-tree typed (mirror of module/interface header pattern). (a) `program_ansi_header` typed `{attributes, lifetime, name, imports, parameters, ports}`. (b) `program_nonansi_header` same field names (only ports source rule differs — list_of_ports vs (list_of_port_declarations)?). For `program p; endprogram\n`: header.name = 'p' (clean string from slice 8), all 6 named fields populated. Annotation count: 67 (was 65, +2). Same accept set.

2026-05-05 SV-Slice-10 (batch): class + package + program declarations typed. (a) `class_declaration_sv_2017` — single sequence typed `{virtual, lifetime, name, parameters, extends, implements, items, end_label}`. (b) `class_declaration_sv_2023` — same shape with `final_specifier` instead of `lifetime` (semantically distinct field per LRM 2023). (c) `package_declaration` — single sequence typed `{attributes, lifetime, name, timeunits, items, end_label}` (annotation registered; runtime parse on bare `package p; endpackage` input fails at position 0 — likely pre-existing issue, not introduced by this slice; module/interface/program tests still pass with same parser). (d) `program_declaration_sv_2017` — 5 per-branch kinds (nonansi/ansi/wildcard/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi). NOTE: program puts nonansi BEFORE ansi in the rule definition (different from module/interface) — kind labels still discriminate correctly. (e) `program_declaration_sv_2023` — same 5 kinds with positional shift in wildcard. Verified empirically on `program p; endprogram\n`: source_text[0].body.body.kind = 'ansi'. Annotation count: 65 (was 53, +12). Same accept set across all annotated rules.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-9 (batch): interface declarations typed — full mirror of the module pattern. (a) `interface_ansi_header` typed `{attributes, lifetime, name, imports, parameters, ports}` (no keyword field — only one keyword exists for interfaces). (b) `interface_nonansi_header` same field names. (c) `interface_declaration_sv_2017` 5 per-branch kinds (ansi/nonansi/wildcard/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi). (d) `interface_declaration_sv_2023` same kind set with positional shift in wildcard. Verified empirically on `interface bus; endinterface\n`: source_text[0].body.body.kind='ansi', header.name='bus' (clean string, inherited from slice 8 identifier typing). Annotation count: 53 (was 41, +12). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-8 (batch): four identifier-leaf rules typed (`-> $2` to drop trivia + surface the regex-captured identifier name as a clean string). (a) `simple_identifier := trivia /[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_$]*/ -> $2`. (b) `escaped_identifier := trivia /\\[!-~]+/ -> $2`. (c) `non_keyword_identifier := !reserved_non_keyword_identifier identifier -> $2`. (d) `simple_identifier_no_scope := trivia /[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_$]*(?![ \t\r\n]*::)/ -> $2`. **Massive propagation effect**: these are leaf rules consumed by `identifier`, `declaration_identifier`, `module_identifier`, `class_identifier`, `package_identifier`, `interface_identifier`, etc. — each of which is a transparent passthrough alias. After this slice, ALL identifier-typed fields (anywhere in any rule that ultimately resolves to an identifier) surface as clean JSON strings instead of the raw envelope chain. For `module m; endmodule\n`: `header.name` was `[[], [[], "m"]]` (raw envelope) → now `"m"` (clean string). Annotation count: 41 (was 37, +4). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-7 (batch): four rules typed in one slice. (a) `module_keyword` per-branch typed (`{kind: "module"}` / `{kind: "macromodule"}`). (b) `lifetime` per-branch typed (`{kind: "static"}` / `{kind: "automatic"}`). (c) `module_ansi_header` typed `{attributes, keyword, lifetime, name, imports, parameters, ports}`. (d) `module_nonansi_header` typed with same field names (only `ports` source differs — `list_of_ports` vs `(list_of_port_declarations)?`). For `module m; endmodule\n`: the previously-raw `header:` field of the ansi-kind module_declaration_sv_2017 now resolves to a typed object with `keyword: {kind: "module"}` (via slice 7a) and named optional/quantified fields. **Four layers of typed dispatch now end-to-end**: source_text_item.kind → description.kind → module_declaration_sv_<profile>.kind → module_<form>_header (with sub-typed keyword). Annotation count: 37 (was 31, +6: 2 module_keyword, 2 lifetime, 1 module_ansi_header, 1 module_nonansi_header). Same accept set across all four slices.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-6 (batch): three rules typed in one slice. (a) `attribute_instance` annotated `{first: $2, rest: $3}` — drops attr_open/close delimiters, exposes first attr_spec + trailing reps. (b) `module_declaration_sv_2017` per-branch typed (5 branches: ansi/nonansi/wildcard/extern_nonansi/extern_ansi) with kind discriminator and structured fields (header/timeunits/items/end_label, plus attributes/keyword/lifetime/name on the wildcard branch). (c) `module_declaration_sv_2023` same per-branch typing as sv_2017 (kind set is identical; only the wildcard branch's positional indices differ — sv_2023 uses `dot star` (2 tokens) vs sv_2017's `dot_star` (1 token), shifting timeunits from $9→$10, items from $10→$11, end_label from $12→$13). Three layers of typed dispatch now end-to-end: source_text_item.kind → description.kind → module_declaration_sv_<profile>.kind. For `module m; endmodule\n` (sv_2017 profile): source_text[0].body.body now resolves to `{kind: "ansi", header: <module_ansi_header envelope>, timeunits: [], items: [], end_label: []}`. Annotation count: 31 (was 20). NOTE: `comment_only_source_region` typing was attempted in this batch but BLOCKED by task #38 (parens-grouped-Or trailing-annotation attribution bug); annotation didn't register and was reverted. Same accept set across all three slices.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-5: `compiler_directive` transparent passthrough of directive text (`-> $2`). For an input with `` `define FOO bar `` followed by `module m; endmodule\n`, source_text now has 2 items: item[0] is `{kind: "compiler_directive", body: "`define FOO bar"}` (body is now a clean string instead of nested envelope), item[1] is the typed module description. Drops the `trivia` prefix slot from the matched sequence; emits just the captured directive text. Annotation count: 20 (was 19). Same accept set; only the compiler_directive shape changed.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-4: `description` per-branch typed (8 branches). Each Or branch annotated. Branches 1-5 + 8 (single-element): `{kind: "<branch_name>", body: $1}`. Branches 6-7 (multi-element `attribute_instance* X`): `{kind: "<branch_name>", attributes: $1, body: $2}` — preserves attribute_instance* prefix as a separate `attributes` field. For the `module m; endmodule\n` sample, source_text[0].body is now `{"kind": "module_declaration", "body": <envelope>}` (was raw envelope). Two layers of typed dispatch now end-to-end (source_text_item.kind → description.kind). Annotation count: 19 (was 11). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-3: `source_text_item` per-branch typed (8 branches). Each Or branch annotated with `{kind: "<branch_name>", body: $1}` — kinds: description, local_parameter_declaration, parameter_declaration, package_import_declaration, timeunits_declaration, compiler_directive, comment_only_source_region, semi (the last carries no body since it's just `;`). Pre-fix each source_text_item carried the matched-branch shape directly without a discriminator; post-fix consumers can dispatch on `item["kind"]`. For the `module m; endmodule\n` sample, source_text[0] is now `{"kind": "description", "body": <description envelope>}`. Annotation count: 11 (was 3). Same accept set.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-2: `source_text := source_text_item*` annotated `-> [$1**]`. Pre-fix `source_text` field of `systemverilog_file` was a nested Quantified envelope; post-fix it's a flat array of `source_text_item` shapes. For the `module m; endmodule\n` sample, source_text now has length 1 (the single module_declaration's source_text_item envelope) instead of the Quantified wrapper. Annotation count: 3 (was 2). Same accept set; only the source_text array shape changed.

2026-05-04 SV-Slice-1: dangling annotation `-> {type: "systemverilog_file", source_text: $2}` at grammars/systemverilog.ebnf line 195 was moved onto its target rule `systemverilog_file` (line 184). Pre-fix the annotation was lost (separated from its rule by a blank line + the `sv_multi_entry_root` helper definition + another blank line) and the parser registered 0 annotations effective on systemverilog_file. Post-fix the parser registers 2 annotations (systemverilog_file + systemverilog_parseable_file) and the AST root for `module m; endmodule\n` is the typed `{type: "systemverilog_file", source_text: ...}` JSON object documented below.
